title: Headphone off-ear detection
abstract: Disclosed is a signal processor for headphone off-ear detection. The signal processor includes an audio output to transmit an audio signal toward a headphone speaker in a headphone cup. The signal processor also includes a feedback (FB) microphone input to receive a FB signal from a FB microphone in the headphone cup. The signal processor also includes an off-ear detection (OED) signal processor to determine an audio frequency response of the FB signal over an OED frame as a received frequency response. The OED processor also determines an audio frequency response of the audio signal times an off-ear transfer function between the headphone speaker and the FB microphone as an ideal off-ear response. A difference metric is generated comparing the received frequency response to the ideal off-ear frequency response. The difference metric is employed to detect when the headphone cup is disengaged from an ear.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A headset having a narrowband off-ear detection system for determining whether the headset is on a userâs ear, the headset comprising: an input configured to receive a headphone audio signal having injected therein a in audible tone signal generated by a tone generator at or above l5KHz; at least one microphone configured to receive a microphone signal; and a narrowband off-ear detection processor configured to determine whether the headset is on the userâs ear based at least in part on the headphone audio signal and the microphone signal. 2. The headset of claim 1 wherein the non-audible tone signal has a frequency that is within a range between 20 KHz and 192 KHz. 3. The headset of claim 1 in which the headphone audio signal also includes a second injected tone signal having a frequency between l5Hz and 30Hz. 4. The headset of claim 1 wherein the narrowband off-ear detection processor is configured to determine whether the headset is on the userâs ear by comparing each of a characteristic of either or both the headphone audio signal and the microphone signal to a corresponding off-ear detection threshold. 5. The headset of claim 4 wherein the characteristic of either or both the headphone audio signal and the microphone signal is a power value or an energy value. 6. An off-ear detection system for determining whether a headset is on a userâs ear, the system comprising: a tone generator configured to inject an in-audible tone signal into a headphone audio signal having a frequency of at or above l5KHz; a first microphone configured to convert an ambient sound wave to a first microphone signal; and a narrowband off-ear detection processor of the headset configured to receive the headphone audio signal with the injected non-audible tone signal and the first microphone 

 signal, and determine whether the headset is on the userâs ear based at least in part on the headphone audio signal and the first microphone signal. 7. The system of claim 6 wherein the narrowband off-ear detection processor is configured to determine whether the headset is on the userâs ear by comparing a characteristic of either or both the headphone audio signal and the first microphone signal to a corresponding off-ear detection threshold. 8. The system of claim 6 further comprising a second microphone configured to convert an ambient sound wave to a second microphone signal. 9. The system of claim 8 wherein the narrowband off-ear detection processor is configured to determine whether the headset is on the userâs ear based at least in part on the second microphone signal. 10. The system of claim 9 wherein the narrowband off-ear detection processor is configured to determine whether the headset is on the userâs ear by comparing a characteristic of any or all of the headphone audio signal, the first microphone signal, and the second microphone signal to a corresponding off-ear detection threshold. 11. The system of claim 6 further comprising a wideband off-ear detection processor configured to determine whether the headset is on the userâs ear based at least in part on certain acoustic changes. 12. The system of claim 11 further comprising a combination circuit configured to compare a first output signal based on the determination as to whether the headset is on the userâs ear by the narrowband off-ear detection processor to a second output signal based on the determination as to whether the headset is on the userâs ear by the wideband off-ear detection processor. 13. The system of claim 6 in which the tone generator is also configured to inject a second tone signal having a frequency between l5Hz and 30Hz into the headphone audio signal. 14. A method for determining whether a headset is on a userâs ear, the method comprising: injecting a non-audible tone signal into a headphone audio signal by a tone generator having a frequency at or above l5KHz; receiving the headphone audio signal with the injected non-audible tone signal by a narrowband off-ear detection processor of the headset; receiving a first microphone signal from a first microphone by the narrowband off-ear detection processor; and determining by the narrowband off-ear detection processor whether the headset is on the userâs ear based at least in part on the headphone audio signal and the first microphone signal. 15. The method of claim 14 wherein the determining includes comparing each of a characteristic of either or both the headphone audio signal and the first microphone signal to a corresponding off-ear detection threshold by the narrowband off-ear detection processor. 16. The method of claim 15 wherein the characteristic of either or both the headphone audio signal and the first microphone signal is a power value or an energy value. 17. The method of claim 14 further comprising receiving a second microphone signal from a second microphone by the narrowband off-ear detection processor. 18. The method of claim 17 wherein the determining includes comparing each of a characteristic of any or all of the headphone audio signal, the first microphone signal, and a second microphone signal to a corresponding off-ear detection threshold by the narrowband off-ear detection processor. 19. The method of claim 14 further comprising injecting a second tone signal into the headphone audio signal by the tone generator having a frequency between l5KHz and 30KHz. 20. The method of claim 14 further comprising filtering the headphone audio signal by a bandpass filter before the headphone audio signal is received by the narrowband off-ear detection processor.
